title: Asuka as the main star
wikitext:
[]: Asuka as the main star             Quote Post by Radical Lanzar » Fri Feb 24, 2012 3:07 am Just read about this while on Wikipedia. Asuka was meant to be the main star, but was deemed by the creators a fallacy of mediocre anime - so she was turned into an idol/diva and Shinji was created as the story's lead instead. I'm just thinking Asuka as the main star, and Shinji as Rei's replacement. Why didn't they do this?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Feb 24, 2012 3:39 am Same reason they got rid of her freckles from the concept art: would have been too awesome.

[]: Factor it out. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Feb 24, 2012 3:44 am A strong, confident female protagonist had been done before, Hayao Miyazaki is sort of famous for it. The show was meant to be in part a deconstruction of Mecha tropes. Having a male still center, and portraying them as a coward does so more subtly and more meaningfully than just swapping the hot-blooded guy with a hot-blooded girl. Further, it was Yoshiyuki Sadamoto's idea that this lead girl have an attitude. My impression is that Hideaki Anno at first wanted a girl lead, but regardless wanted the lead to have a personality like Shinji's in order better reflect the point he was going to make. A faint hearted girl in a mecha series? They're not typically the protagonist, but they're also not new. Ultimately, Mr. Anno must of seen having the protagonist be male better served his purposes.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Feb 24, 2012 10:02 am Alaska Slim nailed it there. Also, having a rather faint hearted boy in the series as a main character would cause more drama (hurts me to say) because he's a boy . If you haven't noticed, the more drama, the better (in Anno's eyes).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 24, 2012 10:35 am Indeed, there's a lot of gender politics going on. The Asuka/Yui show that was proposed would have probably been just disposable nonsense.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Feb 24, 2012 1:39 pm Because Asuka is BORING as a protagonist. Most of her character drama comes from Shinji one-upping her and wrecking her world-view.

[]: Re: Factor it out. Bl             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Feb 24, 2012 1:52 pm This has been discussed somewhere on the forums before; with the essential point being that Anno was argued out of going with another female lead after having immediately previously done so with Nadia. Alaska Slim wrote: The show was meant to be in part a deconstruction of Mecha tropes. Having a male still center, and portraying them as a coward, does so more subtly and more meaningfully than just swapping the hot-blooded guy with a hot-blooded girl.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 24, 2012 2:40 pm I never heard that it was supposed to be Asuka specifically, just "it was going to be a female protagonist, but then... Shinji".

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Feb 24, 2012 3:23 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Because Asuka is BORING as a protagonist. Most of her character drama comes from Shinji one-upping her and wrecking her world-view.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Feb 25, 2012 12:03 pm I'd really like a complete sex swap in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Feb 25, 2012 12:50 pm "You see, the Evangelions are actually...your fathers." "...That...doesn't seem very symboli-" "SHUT THE FUCK UP AND GET IN THE EVA."

[]:             Quote Post by Radical Lanzar » Sat Feb 25, 2012 4:09 pm AuraTwilight wrote: "You see, the Evangelions are actually...your fathers." "...That...doesn't seem very symboli-" "SHUT THE FUCK UP AND GET IN THE EVA."

[]:             Quote Post by BulletShoot » Mon Feb 27, 2012 3:56 pm If Asuka had been the main character, it's seems like it would either have become Nadia or it would have become Gunbuster, and while both of those are fine series, I believe the series is better off the way it turned out (at least in that regard).

[]:             Quote Post by Kamon-san » Tue Feb 28, 2012 9:49 am I'd really like a complete sex swap in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Feb 28, 2012 3:00 pm The real problem is, to make Asuka the main character, you'd have to do more than have the events we saw in the series from a different viewpoint -- we lose both the start of the Angel invasion and its culmination; and while flashbacks could handle some of that, the "what this story is about" isn't set up for her.

[]:             Quote Post by Radical Lanzar » Tue Feb 28, 2012 6:22 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The real problem is, to make Asuka the main character, you'd have to do more than have the events we saw in the series from a different viewpoint -- we lose both the start of the Angel invasion and its culmination; and while flashbacks could handle some of that, the "what this story is about" isn't set up for her.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Feb 28, 2012 6:54 pm By "what the story is about", I meant this

[]:             Quote Post by Radical Lanzar » Tue Feb 28, 2012 8:17 pm Mr. Tines wrote: By "what the story is about", I meant this

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Tue Feb 28, 2012 8:59 pm Sex change would be totally boring. Make the feminine character female and the masculine character male? Instantly transform hot yuri bathouse scene into uncomfortable yaoi scene? Somehow convey Asuka bending over in a bikini top with the use of a speedo? Hmm. If you wanted to keep things from being completely ridiculous, you'd probably want to just flip the roles of Misato and Kaji, rather than genderswap then. Then Oscar can awkwardly come on to Misato on the Over the Rainbow. Yay.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 29, 2012 3:15 am arkiel wrote: Instantly transform hot yuri bathouse scene into uncomfortable yaoi scene?
